questions.stem: A developer creates an agent file but the parser reports an error about missing required sections. Which two sections are required in every ABL agent document?

options.text: AGENT: and GOAL: -- the agent name and its primary objective
AGENT: and EXECUTION: -- the agent name and the LLM model configuration
AGENT: and FLOW: -- the agent name and the step-based execution definition

questions.explanation: Every ABL agent document must contain AGENT: (the agent name) and GOAL: (the agent's objective). All other sections -- including EXECUTION, FLOW, PERSONA, TOOLS, and others -- are optional. If EXECUTION is omitted, the platform applies built-in defaults.

questions.stem: ABL agent definition files use the file extension ________.
questions.explanation: ABL uses .agent.abl as the standard file extension for agent definitions. Reusable tool libraries use .tools.abl, and YAML-format agent definitions use .agent.yaml. Both ABL and YAML formats produce the same intermediate representation.

questions.stem: A team wants multiple agents to share the same hotel search API tool. What is the recommended approach in ABL?

options.text: Copy and paste the tool definition into each agent file individually
Define the tools in a shared .tools.abl file and import them using FROM ... USE syntax
Define all agents in a single .agent.abl file so they automatically share tools

questions.explanation: ABL supports reusable tool files (.tools.abl) that can be shared across agents. You define tools with shared configuration (base_url, auth, timeout, retry) in a .tools.abl file, then import specific tools using 'FROM "./path/to/file.tools.abl" USE: tool_name' syntax in each agent that needs them.

questions.stem: In ABL syntax, section keywords like AGENT:, GOAL:, and TOOLS: follow which convention?

options.text: They must be written in lowercase and are case-sensitive
They are case-insensitive at the parser level, but uppercase is the canonical convention
They must alternate between uppercase and lowercase depending on the section type

questions.explanation: Section keywords are uppercase by convention and followed by a colon. The parser is case-insensitive -- AGENT:, agent:, and Agent: all parse identically. However, uppercase is the canonical style for ABL format files. In YAML format files (.agent.yaml), lowercase keys are used exclusively.

questions.stem: ABL supports template interpolation using double-brace syntax, such as "Hello, {{customer_name}}." These expressions are resolved at ________ against session variables and tool results.
questions.explanation: Template expressions using double-brace syntax (e.g., {{customer_name}}, {{balance}}) are resolved at runtime against session variables and tool results. ABL also supports Handlebars-style conditional blocks like {{#if exchange_rate}}.
